phases.what: Confirm dates, city, headcount, budget expectations
Hotel + budget approved by Mar 25, WeWork + social activity booked by Mar 27
Coordinate agenda with leadership, collect materials
Send pre-reads to team (1 week before)

phase1_kickoff.item: Confirm dates and city — April 20-21, NYC
Confirm headcount and attendee list — 9 attendees
Create a Planning Hub page for this offsite
Create calendar event (attendees in notes, no invites sent yet)

hotel.item: Collect Marriott member numbers (offer to link reservations)
Send hotel confirmation numbers to attendees

wework.item: Find nearest WeWork to hotel (walking distance)
Book conference room or private office for the team (capacity 10+)

socialActivity.item: Book dinner restaurant (pair with social activity)

food.item: Prepare DoorDash group order links in advance — each day

budget.item: Compile estimated costs across all categories
Draft budget approval request to Evelyn
Get budget approved before committing to bookings
Track committed and actual spend as bookings are made

phase3_agenda.item: Review #leadership Slack channel (past 30 days) for discussion themes
Run keyword searches across Slack (strategy, roadmap, priority, blocker, hiring, revenue)
Check Granola meeting notes for recent leadership topics
Coordinate with Evelyn and leadership team on agenda topics and materials
Confirm topic owners and time slots with leadership
Build agenda in Notion based on leadership input
Collect pre-read materials from topic owners
Send pre-reads to team — by Mon Apr 13 (1 week before)

phase4_finalComms.item: Draft logistics announcement for #leadership (get approval before sending)
Send hotel confirmation details to attendees
Follow up with anyone who hasn't shared travel details
Confirm all bookings (hotel, venue, activity, restaurant)
Build buffer time into schedule for unstructured socializing

dayBefore.item: Prepare DoorDash links for lunch orders

day1.item: Send DoorDash link at start of first session

postOffsite.item: Capture lessons learned — update checklist for next time
Follow up on action items and owners

hotelResearch.name: Courtyard by Marriott Midtown East
hotelResearch.pros: 5-min walk to WeWork 575 Fifth Ave & 430 Park Ave. Modern rooms, free Wi-Fi, 12th-floor lounge. Close to subway (4/5/6).
hotelResearch.bestPairedWith: WeWork 575 Fifth Ave or 430 Park Ave

hotelResearch.name: SpringHill Suites Midtown Manhattan
hotelResearch.pros: Large rooms by NYC standards, free breakfast included. Walk to WeWork 1460 Broadway. Near Grand Central.

hotelResearch.name: Renaissance New York Midtown
hotelResearch.pros: 348 rooms, floor-to-ceiling windows. Walk to WeWork Midtown West and Penn Station.

hotelResearch.name: Moxy NYC Chelsea
hotelResearch.pros: Fermat has automatic 10% off website rate. WeWork across the street. Alice (travel agent) negotiating further with GM Diana.
hotelResearch.aliceNote: Alice contacted Diana at Moxy for Fermat-specific discount. $1,120/room for both nights before discount. Awaiting final pricing.

hotel.name: Moxy NYC Chelsea (pending final pricing)
hotel.rateNote: ~$420/room with 10% Fermat discount, pending Diana confirmation via Alice

weworkResearch.name: 575 Fifth Avenue
weworkResearch.notes: Near Grand Central, Bryant Park. B/D/F/M/4/5/6/7 trains.

weworkResearch.name: 430 Park Avenue
weworkResearch.notes: Quieter location, close to Grand Central.

venue.name: WeWork (booked)
venue.costNote: Day pass conference rooms start ~$10/seat/hr. 10-person room for 2 full days.

dinnerResearch.name: The Modern
dinnerResearch.notes: Fine dining at MoMA. Impressive setting for a special occasion.

dinnerResearch.name: BLACKBARN
dinnerResearch.vegetarianOptions: Excellent — seasonal vegetarian dishes built into menu
dinnerResearch.notes: Locally sourced, relaxed but upscale. Multiple private spaces.

dinnerResearch.name: Yara
dinnerResearch.notes: Great for diverse dietary needs. Fun shared-plates format like Denver dinner.

activityResearch.name: Escape Room (Escape Room Madness)
activityResearch.why: Proven hit from Denver. Great for problem-solving together.

activityResearch.name: Cooking Class / Great Guac Off
activityResearch.why: Relaxed, social, flexible participation. Fun competitive twist.

activityResearch.name: Improv Workshop (UCB Theatre)
activityResearch.why: Unique NYC experience. Builds communication & quick thinking.

activity.name: TBD
activity.lesson: Group activity + dinner is a proven combo. Escape room was a hit in Denver.

categories.notes: DoorDash group orders x2 days + dinner

themes.title: AI-First Transformation
themes.description: 10x contributor expectations, 3.5x engineering velocity, tool adoption training program. Rishabh shared strategy doc on 3/10.

themes.title: Outcome Ownership Framework
themes.description: Major gap: only Isabel completed the ownership exercise. Need 3-5 outcomes per leader defined.

themes.title: Hiring & Team Composition
themes.description: Bharat: 'how we hire is going to change a lot on eng.' Expect 30% reduction in 6 months. Culture favors high-curiosity.

themes.title: Enterprise Relationships & Account Management
themes.description: Tinuiti model discussion, AM role assignment, Jesse's role. Jess flagged need for clarity.

themes.title: Performance Reviews & Feedback Structure
themes.description: Saunder raised question about sharing packets with departing team members. Feedback structure discussion.

themes.title: Goal Alignment
themes.description: Shared vs. individual goals confusion. Multiple people assigned same goals (e.g., 'O'Keefe native').

themes.title: Speed & Execution
themes.description: Hard to set timelines during pivot, but speed = differentiator. 'Feeling of success' trap.

themes.title: AI for Customer-Facing Teams
themes.description: Jess: 'I get the how does Fermat incorporate AI question all the time.' Could be good for customer teams to have on hand.

lessonsLearned: Prioritize central/downtown location walkable to all activities. Avoid airport-area hotels.
Group activity + dinner is a proven combo. Escape room was a hit. Find unique activities.
Send DoorDash links at the start of sessions so food arrives on time.
Build flexible scheduling — not every minute needs to be programmed. The team valued unstructured bonding time.
Agenda content and topics come from Evelyn and leadership — Sophie coordinates and organizes, but doesn't set the agenda.

coordinationContacts.name: Alice Zhao
coordinationContacts.reachOutFor: Hotel bookings, group rates, flight coordination

aliceCorrespondence.summary: Sophie initially asked about Courtyard Midtown East for 4 rooms. Alice suggested Moxy NYC Chelsea (Fermat has auto 10% off). Moxy quoted ~$1,120/room for both nights without discount. Alice contacted Diana (GM) for Fermat-specific pricing. Sophie confirmed Moxy is the likely choice pending budget approval. Alice is standing by for names, Marriott numbers, and which CC to use once approval comes through.
aliceCorrespondence.nextStep: Awaiting Moxy pricing from Diana via Alice. Need budget approval from Evelyn before committing.
